name: Marion Williams
abstract: Marion Williams (August 29, 1927 – July 2, 1994) was an American gospel singer.

concerts.label: Ninth Annual WLIB Festival of Negro Music and Drama
Midnight Performance: Jazz Stars at Midnight
Newport Jazz Festival: Tribute to Mahalia Jackson

works.t: Standing Here Wondering Which Way to Go
(If You Live Right) Heaven Belongs to You
God Be With You Till We Meet Again

about_quotes.speaker: Junior Mance Part I
about_quotes.evidence: they threw another curve at me... Marion Williams... they were making, it was strictly a Gospel album... Bill Fischer... he did like just some simple charts... I would just do it without thinking. I says, oh, boy, every time they had to do another take it was my fault.
about_quotes.doc_title: Junior Mance Part I interviewed by Monk Rowe and Michael Woods, New York City, New York, July 27, 1995 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Jon Jang
about_quotes.evidence: I forgot where I heard Marion Williams, when I used to perform in an African-American church that was a song that was sung and tears were streaming down people’s faces.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jon Jang interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, September 17, 2003 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Peter Vacher
about_quotes.evidence: Max Jones would review and would enthuse about everyone from Marion Williams and the Stars of Faith... They were all part of a piece as far as we were concerned.
about_quotes.doc_title: Peter Vacher interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clearwater Beach, Florida, March 18, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Helen Humes
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Yeah. Now we just mentioned Marion Williams. That's a different matter. [INTERVIEWEE] Yeah, well I think she's a wonderful gospel singer.
